doctors.defining: The War Games — tried by his own people.

doctors.catchphrase: Reverse the polarity of the neutron flow.
Would you like a jelly baby?
doctors.defining: Genesis of the Daleks — "Have I that right?"
The Caves of Androzani — regenerated saving Peri.

doctors.catchphrase: I am the Doctor — whether you like it or not.
doctors.defining: Trial of a Time Lord — the show on trial inside the show.

doctors.catchphrase: Time will tell. It always does.
doctors.defining: Remembrance of the Daleks — the Hand of Omega.
The Night of the Doctor — eight minutes, eight years of waiting.
The Day of the Doctor — "No sir. All thirteen!"
Dalek (2005) — one Dalek, one cliffhanger, a whole reboot.

doctors.catchphrase: Allons-y! / I'm sorry. I'm so sorry.

doctors.defining: Journey's End — left in Pete's World with Rose.
The Pandorica Opens / The Big Bang — "I am the Doctor."
Heaven Sent — 4.5 billion years in a confession dial.
The Timeless Children — "I'm the Timeless Child."

doctors.catchphrase: Have you ever been told you talk too much?
doctors.defining: Fugitive of the Judoon — the Doctor before the Doctor.
The Giggle — the bigeneration with Fifteen.
The Devil's Chord — sings a Beatles song into the universe.
The Day of the Doctor — "You know I really think you might."

companions.name: Susan Foreman
companions.origin: The Doctor's granddaughter, hiding in 1963 London.
companions.fate: Doctor locked her out of the TARDIS so she'd stay with David Campbell on 22nd-century Earth. "One day, I shall come back." He didn't.

companions.name: Barbara Wright
companions.origin: Coal Hill School history teacher who followed Susan home.
companions.fate: Home to 1965 London with Ian, via a Dalek time machine.

companions.name: Ian Chesterton
companions.origin: Coal Hill School science teacher; Barbara's colleague.
companions.fate: Same Dalek time machine, same 1965 London. Later: Chairman of the Governors of Coal Hill, knighted in 2022.

companions.name: Vicki
companions.origin: Crashed colonist child on Dido.
companions.fate: Left to be Cressida in the fall of Troy.

companions.name: Steven Taylor
companions.origin: Crashed space pilot held captive by the Mechonoids.
companions.fate: Stayed to rule the Elders / Savages.

companions.name: Jamie McCrimmon
companions.origin: Highlander piper, Battle of Culloden 1746.
companions.fate: Time Lords wiped his memory and sent him back to Culloden.

companions.name: Zoe Heriot
companions.origin: Astrophysicist on the Wheel in Space, 21st century.
companions.fate: Memory-wiped by the Time Lords, sent home.

companions.name: Liz Shaw
companions.origin: Cambridge scientist seconded to UNIT.
companions.fate: Went back to Cambridge. Bored of being handed test tubes.

companions.name: Jo Grant
companions.origin: UNIT secretary who failed her A-levels and was assigned to the Doctor anyway.
companions.fate: Married Professor Cliff Jones and went up the Amazon.

companions.name: Sarah Jane Smith
companions.origin: Journalist who sneaked into UNIT looking for a story.
companions.fate: Dropped off in "Croydon" (Aberdeen). Found again in School Reunion. Got her own spin-off until 2011.

companions.name: Harry Sullivan
companions.origin: Naval surgeon at UNIT.
companions.fate: Stayed on Earth after Terror of the Zygons.

companions.name: Leela
companions.origin: Sevateem warrior on a far-future colony world.
companions.fate: Stayed on Gallifrey with Andred. (Big Finish has many opinions.)

companions.name: Romana I
companions.origin: Time Lady from Gallifrey, assigned by the White Guardian.
companions.fate: Regenerated.

companions.name: Romana II
companions.origin: Same Time Lady, after a regeneration that picked a body like Princess Astra's.
companions.fate: Stayed in E-Space to free the Tharils.

companions.name: K-9
companions.origin: Built in 5000 AD by Professor Marius.
companions.fate: Multiple. There are at least four K-9s. The Doctor builds Sarah Jane a new one in School Reunion.

companions.name: Adric
companions.origin: Alzarian boy from E-Space.
companions.fate: Died crashing a Cyber-freighter into prehistoric Earth (it became the Yucatán impact). The show ran the credits in silence.

companions.name: Nyssa
companions.origin: Princess of Traken; her father's body was stolen by the Master.
companions.fate: Stayed on Terminus to cure Lazar's Disease.

companions.name: Tegan Jovanka
companions.origin: Australian air stewardess on her first day, took the wrong door.
companions.fate: Left in tears after Resurrection of the Daleks. Came back in Power of the Doctor with cancer she's beating.

companions.name: Turlough
companions.origin: Trion political exile hiding at an English public school.
companions.fate: Went home to Trion when the political prisoners were amnestied.

companions.name: Peri Brown
companions.origin: American botany student on holiday in Lanzarote.
companions.fate: Trial of a Time Lord said she died; revised mid-trial to say she married King Yrcanos and lived. Big Finish unmoored.

companions.name: Mel Bush
companions.origin: Computer programmer from Pease Pottage.
companions.fate: Wandered off with Sabalom Glitz in Dragonfire. Brought back in Power of the Doctor and now Fifteen's UNIT crew (Empire of Death).

companions.name: Ace
companions.origin: Perivale teenager flung to Iceworld by a time storm she half-caused.
companions.fate: Show ended mid-arc; novels send her to Gallifrey to train. Power of the Doctor: ex-companion in a leather jacket falling out of a CSO sky.

companions.name: Grace Holloway
companions.origin: San Francisco cardiologist on New Year's Eve 1999.
companions.fate: Stayed.

companions.name: Rose Tyler
companions.origin: Powell Estate shop girl, 19, mum on the council.
companions.fate: Trapped in Pete's World twice. Got the Meta-Crisis Doctor as a parting gift.

companions.name: Mickey Smith
companions.origin: Powell Estate, Rose's boyfriend, eaten by a wheelie bin in his first episode.
companions.fate: Married Martha. Last seen fighting Sontarans in End of Time.

companions.name: Captain Jack Harkness
companions.origin: 51st-century Time Agent / con artist, Boeshane Peninsula.
companions.fate: Made immortal by Rose-Bad-Wolf. Runs Torchwood. Will become the Face of Boe.

companions.name: Martha Jones
companions.origin: Royal Hope medical student, North London family.
companions.fate: Walked away with her dignity intact (more than the Doctor ever offered Rose). Married Mickey. UNIT doctor.

companions.name: Donna Noble
companions.origin: Temp from Chiswick, on her wedding day.
companions.fate: DoctorDonna metacrisis burned her up; memory wiped. 2023 returned — her daughter Rose let it out safely.

companions.name: Wilfred Mott
companions.origin: Donna's grandad. Newspaper seller.
companions.fate: Four knocks. Took Ten's regeneration in End of Time. Returned for Wild Blue Yonder before Cribbins's death.

companions.name: Amy Pond
companions.origin: Leadworth, age 7, raggedy man comes through her shed.
companions.fate: Touched by a Weeping Angel into 1938 New York.

companions.name: Rory Williams
companions.origin: Leadworth nurse, Amy's fiancé.
companions.fate: Same Angel, same New York. Lived a full life and died of old age.

companions.name: River Song
companions.origin: Melody Pond. Daughter of Amy and Rory, conceived in the TARDIS, raised by the Silence to kill the Doctor.
companions.fate: Library hard drive in Forest of the Dead — first met him, last for her. Husbands of River Song: their last night, 24 years long.

companions.name: Clara Oswald
companions.origin: Coal Hill School English teacher. Also: scattered through the Doctor's timeline by stepping into his timestream.
companions.fate: Heaven Sent / Hell Bent: died facing the Quantum Shade, lifted out between heartbeats by Twelve. Now travels with Ashildr in a TARDIS "the long way round to Gallifrey."

companions.name: Danny Pink
companions.origin: Coal Hill maths teacher, ex-soldier. Clara's boyfriend.
companions.fate: Hit by a car. Cyber-converted. Gave his afterlife to bring back the boy he killed in Iraq.

companions.name: Bill Potts
companions.origin: University canteen worker; the Doctor was tutoring her on the sly.
companions.fate: Cyber-converted on Floor 1056; rescued by Heather and the puddle as immortal star-stuff.

companions.name: Nardole
companions.origin: Husbands of River Song — a head on a stick.
companions.fate: Stayed on the Mondasian colony ship to guide the survivors.

companions.name: Yasmin Khan
companions.origin: Sheffield police officer, second-generation British-Pakistani.
companions.fate: Walked away in Power of the Doctor after declining to keep waiting.

companions.name: Ryan Sinclair
companions.origin: Sheffield apprentice mechanic with dyspraxia.
companions.fate: Stayed home to help Yaz's grandfather and look after Earth.

companions.name: Graham O'Brien
companions.origin: Sheffield. Retired bus driver. Cancer survivor. Married to Ryan's grandmother Grace.
companions.fate: Stayed with Ryan.

companions.name: Dan Lewis
companions.origin: Liverpool food-bank volunteer.
companions.fate: Walked away after the Sea Devils, in love and unable to keep up the cosmic pace.

companions.name: Ruby Sunday
companions.origin: Abandoned on the steps of a church on Ruby Road one snowy Christmas Eve, 2004.
companions.fate: Found her birth mother. Stayed on Earth — but only just.

companions.name: Belinda Chandra
companions.origin: Croydon nurse who only wants to go home.
companions.fate: Currently travelling.

companions.name: Kate Lethbridge-Stewart
companions.origin: Daughter of Brigadier Alistair Gordon Lethbridge-Stewart. Took over UNIT.
companions.fate: Active. Survived Cyber-conversion in Power of the Doctor.

companions.name: Brigadier Alistair Gordon Lethbridge-Stewart
companions.origin: British Army; founded UNIT.
companions.fate: Died off-screen during Eleven's run ("Wind of Change"). Returned as a Cyberman to salute Twelve in Death in Heaven.

monsters.name: Daleks
monsters.defeatedBy: Their own purity protocols, every time. Bracewell. The Hand of Omega. The Moment.

monsters.name: Cybermen
monsters.defeatedBy: Gold (classic), emotional override (modern), the Master (Doctor Falls).

monsters.name: Weeping Angels
monsters.defeatedBy: Quantum lock — keep them under observation by anything sentient, forever.

monsters.name: The Silence
monsters.defeatedBy: Hypnotic suggestion: "You should kill us all on sight."

monsters.name: Sontarans
monsters.defeatedBy: A well-aimed shot to the probic vent.

monsters.name: Headless Monks
monsters.firstStory: A Good Man Goes to War

episodes.title: An Unearthly Child
episodes.significance: The first episode. The TARDIS leaves the junkyard at 76 Totter's Lane.

episodes.title: The Daleks
episodes.significance: Daleks debut, in episode two. The show was made.

episodes.title: The Aztecs
episodes.significance: Establishes "You can't change history. Not one line."

episodes.title: The Tenth Planet
episodes.significance: First regeneration. First Cybermen.

episodes.title: The Tomb of the Cybermen
episodes.significance: Patrick Troughton at his best; sole surviving Troughton Cyber-serial.

episodes.title: The War Games
episodes.significance: Time Lords named for the first time. The Doctor is tried by his own people.

episodes.title: Spearhead from Space
episodes.significance: First colour serial. UNIT era proper.

episodes.title: Inferno
episodes.significance: Parallel-Earth fascism episode.

episodes.title: Terror of the Autons
episodes.significance: The Master's first appearance.

episodes.title: Genesis of the Daleks
episodes.significance: Origin of the Daleks. Davros debut. "Have I that right?"

episodes.title: Pyramids of Mars
episodes.significance: Best classic-era horror tone. Setup paid off 49 years later in Empire of Death.

episodes.title: The Deadly Assassin
episodes.significance: First Gallifrey serial without companions. Cemented Time Lord lore.

episodes.title: City of Death
episodes.significance: Co-written by Douglas Adams. Highest-rated classic Who episode.

episodes.title: Logopolis
episodes.significance: Tom Baker's regeneration.

episodes.title: Earthshock
episodes.significance: Cybermen return after seven years. Adric dies. Credits run in silence.

episodes.title: The Caves of Androzani
episodes.significance: Davison's regeneration. Often voted #1 classic Who episode of all time.

episodes.title: Vengeance on Varos
episodes.significance: Reality-TV satire 15 years before reality TV.

episodes.title: Remembrance of the Daleks
episodes.significance: Coal Hill 1963. Ace destroys a Dalek with a baseball bat.

episodes.title: The Curse of Fenric
episodes.significance: Peak Cartmel Masterplan. Ace gets a proper backstory.

episodes.title: Survival
episodes.significance: Last classic-era serial. Ends with the Doctor's voice over a sunset.

episodes.title: Doctor Who (The TV Movie)
episodes.significance: Paul McGann's one TV outing.

episodes.title: The Night of the Doctor
episodes.significance: McGann returns for seven minutes; turns the War Doctor canon.

episodes.title: Rose
episodes.significance: The show comes back. RTD's reboot. 10 million viewers.

episodes.title: Dalek
episodes.significance: Single Dalek in a basement. Nine breaks.

episodes.title: The Empty Child / The Doctor Dances
episodes.significance: "Are you my mummy?" Steven Moffat's first script.

episodes.title: The Parting of the Ways
episodes.significance: Eccleston's regeneration. Bad Wolf reveal.

episodes.title: The Christmas Invasion
episodes.significance: Tennant's first proper episode.

episodes.title: School Reunion
episodes.significance: Sarah Jane returns. Sets up her spin-off.

episodes.title: The Girl in the Fireplace
episodes.significance: Steven Moffat one-shot. Madame de Pompadour and the clockwork droids.

episodes.title: Doomsday
episodes.significance: Daleks vs Cybermen. Rose says goodbye on Bad Wolf Bay.

episodes.title: Blink
episodes.significance: Weeping Angels debut. The Doctor barely in his own episode.

episodes.title: Human Nature / The Family of Blood
episodes.significance: The Doctor hides as a human and forgets.

episodes.title: Utopia / The Sound of Drums / Last of the Time Lords
episodes.significance: Master returns. Year That Never Was.

episodes.title: Silence in the Library / Forest of the Dead
episodes.significance: First appearance of River Song. Her ending, his beginning.

episodes.title: Midnight
episodes.significance: Cheap episode forced by budget cuts; one of the best.

episodes.title: Turn Left
episodes.significance: What if Donna had turned right? Rose returns.

episodes.title: The Stolen Earth / Journey's End
episodes.significance: Every modern companion in one room. DoctorDonna.

episodes.title: The End of Time
episodes.significance: Tennant's regeneration. Four knocks.

episodes.title: The Eleventh Hour
episodes.significance: Matt Smith's debut.

episodes.title: Vincent and the Doctor
episodes.significance: Tony Curran as Vincent van Gogh.

episodes.title: The Pandorica Opens / The Big Bang
episodes.significance: Series 5 finale. The Pandorica is built for the Doctor.

episodes.title: The Doctor's Wife
episodes.significance: Neil Gaiman script. The TARDIS speaks.

episodes.title: A Good Man Goes to War
episodes.significance: Melody Pond reveal.

episodes.title: The Angels Take Manhattan
episodes.significance: Goodbye Ponds.

episodes.title: An Adventure in Space and Time
episodes.significance: BBC docudrama about the making of Doctor Who, 1963. David Bradley as William Hartnell.

episodes.title: The Day of the Doctor
episodes.significance: 50th anniversary. Three Doctors. War Doctor canonised. Gallifrey saved.

episodes.title: The Time of the Doctor
episodes.significance: Eleven's regeneration. Trenzalore. New regeneration cycle.

episodes.title: Listen
episodes.significance: Moffat's best psychological one. Or scariest. Same thing.

episodes.title: Heaven Sent
episodes.significance: Capaldi solo. 55 minutes inside the confession dial. 4.5 billion years.

episodes.title: Hell Bent
episodes.significance: Gallifrey returns. Clara escapes.

episodes.title: Heaven Sent / Hell Bent / Face the Raven
episodes.significance: Series 9 finale trilogy. Clara dies, then escapes.

episodes.title: World Enough and Time / The Doctor Falls
episodes.significance: Mondasian Cybermen origin. Missy and Master duel.

episodes.title: Twice Upon a Time
episodes.significance: Capaldi's regeneration. First Doctor cameo (David Bradley).

episodes.title: The Woman Who Fell to Earth
episodes.significance: Jodie Whittaker's debut.

episodes.title: Demons of the Punjab
episodes.significance: Partition of India, 1947. Yaz's grandmother.

episodes.title: Spyfall
episodes.significance: Sacha Dhawan's Master debut. Gallifrey destroyed (again).

episodes.title: Fugitive of the Judoon
episodes.significance: Jo Martin's Fugitive Doctor. Canon explodes.

episodes.title: The Timeless Children
episodes.significance: Timeless Child reveal. Doctor predates Gallifrey.

episodes.title: Flux
episodes.significance: Six-part serial. The Flux unmakes the universe.

episodes.title: The Power of the Doctor
episodes.significance: Whittaker's regeneration. Classic companions return. Bigeneration set up.

episodes.title: The Star Beast
episodes.significance: Tennant returns. Meep. DoctorDonna problem.

episodes.title: Wild Blue Yonder
episodes.significance: Two-hander on the edge of the universe.

episodes.title: The Giggle
episodes.significance: Bigeneration. Two Doctors at once.

episodes.title: The Church on Ruby Road
episodes.significance: Gatwa's first proper episode.

episodes.title: The Devil's Chord
episodes.significance: 1963 Abbey Road. Music returns to the world.

episodes.title: Boom
episodes.significance: Moffat returns. Doctor stands on a landmine.

episodes.title: Empire of Death
episodes.significance: Sutekh returns after 49 years. Has been on the TARDIS the whole time.

episodes.title: The Robot Revolution
episodes.significance: Belinda debut.

episodes.title: Lux
episodes.significance: Mr Ring-a-Ding. Animated villain.
